title: Jedi Apprentice: The Call to Vengeance
content:
{{Top|rwm|imp|italics=1}} {{Book |image=[[File:JA16Call_To_Vengeance.jpg]] |title=''Jedi Apprentice: The Call to Vengeance'' |author=[[Jude Watson]]''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' |cover artist=[[Cliff Nielsen]] |illustrator= |editor= |publisher=[[Scholastic]] |early release= |release date=[[December]] [[2001]] |intl release= |rereleased= |media type=Paperback |pages= |isbn=0 439 13935 X |isbn2= |isbn3= |timeline=[[41 BBY/Legends|41 BBY]] |series=''[[Star Wars: Jedi Apprentice]]'' |preceded by=[[Jedi Apprentice: The Death of Hope|''The Death of Hope'']] |followed by=[[Jedi Apprentice: The Only Witness|''The Only Witness'']] }} '''''Jedi Apprentice: The Call to Vengeance''''' is the sixteenth in a series of young reader novels that chronicle the adventures of [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]] and [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] prior to [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']]. ==Publisher's summary==
'''Peace over Anger
Honor over Hate
Strength over Fear'''
''Qui-Gon Jinn is a man on the edge. His heart has been shattered. His beliefs have been destroyed. And now he is dangerously close to stepping over a line that no Jedi dares to cross.'' ''He wants revenge.'' ''His fellow Jedi—his apprentice Obi-Wan Kenobi, and Jedi Council member Mace Windu—will try to stop him. But when Qui-Gon strikes out on his own, anything can happen. Revenge is not a Jedi trait…but it is a Human one. Can Qui-Gon separate his personal feelings from his Jedi training?'' ==Plot summary== Word of [[Tahl]]’s shocking [[Death/Legends|death]] and the instability of [[New Apsolon/Legends|New Apsolon]] brings [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]]. [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn's]] heart is not in his work. After the death of [[Tahl|his truest love]], he is finding it hard to continue as a [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]]. He grapples with the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] as his desire for revenge against [[Balog (Human)|Balog]] dominates his thoughts. When he and his [[Padawan/Legends|apprentice]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan]] are sent to a hostile planet to protect the only witness willing to testify against an evil crime family, he finds it hard to focus. But when the witness is suddenly under attack and the crime family makes a move to ensure its survival, Qui-Gon must keep the peace...and fight both the foes who attack and the enemies within. When he finally catches his quarry, he resists temptation. The [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] successfully thwart the coup engineered by the radical [[Absolutes|absolute government]], allowing New Apsolon to continue on a more honest path in redefining its government. ==Media== ===Cover gallery=== This is a gallery of the different cover variations of ''Jedi Apprentice: The Call to Vengeance''. File:JediApprentice_16_typo.jpg|Preliminary cover with typo ("Vengence") File:JA16Call_To_Vengeance.jpg|Paperback File:JediApprentice_16_De.jpg|German - [[:de:Der Schrei nach Vergeltung|''Jedi-Padawan: Der Schrei nach Vergeltung'']] File:JediApprentice_16_Fr.jpg|French - ''Les apprentis Jedi 16: Désir de Vengeance'' File:JediApprentice_16_Es.jpg|Spanish - ''Aprendiz de
